description:
Goal: compiler2 owns its type world end-to-end. A type name is an identity resolved to a hard Ty by a DEMANDED fact, exactly like an MFA; once a hard Ty is in hand, only the shared no-risk algebra (crate::types) and spec engine (crate::specs) are used. Sever the reincorporation of old-world naming -- the per-call ModuleTypeEnv rebuilt by re-lexing runtime source (function_type_env -> runtime_library::{root_type_env,interfaces}), the eager protocol-domain union, and the name-keyed Nominals leak. None of these match the northstar; they were copied in wholesale.

Signal: (1) the drive lexer.pass count equals the number of distinct sources -- the ~117 redundant re-lexes per compile that quicksort.log exposed are structurally gone and observably so; (2) the 150 compiler2 drive_test oracles stay green (reached frontier, Enumerable dispatch selection, unused_runtime_library_stays_cold, JIT/interp runs); (3) DeriveTypeDef (the expensive type-algebra) runs only for type names a reached consumer actually reads -- a @type no reached spec references stays cold like an uncalled function.

Strategy: one decision in the right place -- type names ride the namespace + fact rails that already carry MFAs (reference_type mirrors reference_function; DeriveTypeDef mirrors DeriveFunctionContract; the work graph is the fixpoint). That makes the redundant work unrepresentable, so the inches are mostly deletions. The shared kernel (crate::type_expr, runtime_library, ModuleTypeEnv) is UNTOUCHED -- it stays for the retiring legacy fz frontend and dies with it. Boundary discriminator: do we have the hard Ty yet? Before = in-house; after = shared.

Design captured present-tense in .agent/targets/{type-naming,type-world,set-theoretic-types,protocols}.md and the northstar Type Naming And The Surface Tier section; promoted to .agent/docs/ at the end.

Laces into fz-rh2 as a blocker of fz-rh2.9 (old compiler scrap): compiler2 must stand alone on in-house naming before the old world is scrapped. The terminal child returns to the original quicksort.log perf question -- by then solved by construction.

comments.text:
Sequencing (one decision -> mostly deletions; the work graph is the fixpoint):

```mermaid
graph LR
    1[".1 note names + reference_type"] --> 2[".2 DeriveTypeDef + TypeDefined"]
    2 --> 3[".3 in-house resolver"]
    2 --> 6[".6 inner-in-symbol kernel"]
    3 --> 4[".4 re-seat consumers; delete function_type_env"]
    3 --> 5[".5 protocol domain = marker"]
    4 --> 7[".7 sever + lexer-pass guard"]
    5 --> 7
    6 --> 7
    7 --> 8[".8 promote docs"]
    8 --> 9[".9 back here: re-examine quicksort.log"]
    9 -.lace.-> RH29["fz-rh2.9 scrap old compiler"]
```

Boundary: in-house = name -> hard Ty (.1-.5); shared/untouched = the algebra and spec engine over hard Ty, plus the whole legacy crate::type_expr / runtime_library / ModuleTypeEnv (kept for the retiring fz frontend). Removes more than it adds.

Design refinement (from the @spec to FunctionContract parity discussion):

The lifecycle is Note / Reference / Resolve, and the reference/resolve split is the load-bearing invariant -- it is what makes the re-lex structurally unrepresentable rather than merely removed.

  - Reference is an INDEX-time walk (.1): every type-position name is minted and recorded as its consumer dep. The recorded set IS the consumer wait-set.
  - Resolve is split across the consumer and the resolver: the CONSUMER (.4) is the demand source -- it waits on its recorded TypeDefined, then calls a PURE-READ resolver (.3) that never blocks, demands, or mints.

So a type name rides one tier below the function that names it: a @spec referencing SomeModule.t pulls DeriveTypeDef only because DeriveFunctionContract waited on TypeDefined -- and a @type no reached consumer references stays cold (epic signal 3, gated in .2). DeriveFunctionContract over MFAs is the exact template.

Unchanged by this: the DAG, the inch boundaries, the lace into fz-rh2.9, and the two-grains contract/body split (already existing compiler2 structure -- a caller reads the contract without entering the body).

title: Compiler2 type naming: bring name→Ty in-house as demanded facts
